meta.description: How SageReasoning applies the source-derived philosophy to evaluate human actions. This is the APPLICATION of the other 7 files, not a source domain itself. V1's methodology separated source content from application content — scoring-rules.json was clearly SageReasoning's design applied to the source material. V3 maintains this separation.
meta.derivation: This file is DERIVED, not directly extracted from a source section. It integrates: the causal sequence from psychology.json, the diagnostic framework from passions.json, the kathekon/katorthoma distinction from action.json, the deliberation framework from action.json, the virtue assessment from virtue.json, and the progress model from progress.json.
meta.note: Unlike the other 7 files, this file represents SageReasoning's practical implementation. The source grounding for each component is documented in the file it references.

evaluation_sequence.description: Every action is evaluated through 4 stages. This sequence mirrors the Stoic causal sequence (psychology.json) and the two-layer action model (action.json) but is specifically designed as a practical evaluation tool.

stages.name: Prohairesis Filter
stages.question: What was and was not within the agent's moral choice (prohairesis)?
stages.description: Before any evaluation, separate what was up to the agent from what was not. Score only what is eph' hemin: judgements, impulses, desires, aversions, character. Do NOT score external outcomes.

stages.name: Appropriate Action Assessment
stages.question: Is this action a kathekon — an appropriate action for which a reasonable justification can be given?
stages.description: Evaluate whether the external action itself makes sense given the agent's nature, relationships (oikeiosis stage), and circumstances. This is the first layer from action.json.
criteria: Does the action accord with the agent's natural relationships? (action.json > oikeiosis_sequence)
Can a reasonable justification be given? (Stobaeus definition in action.json)
Does it serve the roles the agent occupies?
Is it the kind of action nature and reason prescribe?

stages.name: Passion Diagnosis
stages.question: Which passions, if any, distorted the agent's impression, assent, or impulse?
stages.description: The diagnostic core. Instead of scoring 4 virtues independently, diagnose which specific passion(s) corrupted the causal sequence. Each passion detected represents a specific false judgement that can be named and corrected.
stages.diagnostic_sequence: Use the 5-step diagnostic from passions.json > diagnostic_use

output.passions_detected: array of passion IDs with sub-species from passions.json
output.false_judgements: array of the specific false beliefs identified
output.causal_stage_affected: which stage of the causal sequence (psychology.json) was corrupted

stages.name: Unified Virtue Assessment
stages.question: How close is the agent's disposition to the sage ideal?
stages.description: Assess the UNIFIED quality of the ruling faculty as expressed through whichever virtue-domain(s) the action engages. Reference virtue.json unity thesis: a deficiency in any one domain indicates a deficiency in the whole.

evaluation_by_domain.phronesis: Did the agent see the situation clearly? Did they distinguish genuine good from apparent good?
evaluation_by_domain.dikaiosyne: Did the agent give each affected person their due? Did they honour their oikeiosis obligations?
evaluation_by_domain.andreia: Did the agent act rightly despite difficulty or apparent risk?
evaluation_by_domain.sophrosyne: Was the action free from excessive impulse? Was it measured and fitting?

output.ruling_faculty_state: Stable disposition (hexis) or temporary state? Consistent with general character or an exception?

katorthoma_proximity_scale.description: A qualitative scale for how close an action is to the sage's right action (katorthoma). The sources describe a BINARY distinction (sage vs. non-sage) with a GRADIENT of progress within the non-sage category. This scale captures that gradient.

levels.name: Reflexive
levels.description: Action from pure impulse with no deliberation. Passion dominates completely. Multiple false judgements operative.

levels.name: Habitual
levels.description: Action from social convention or habit, not from understanding. May be a kathekon externally but performed without knowledge of why it is right.
levels.progress_grade: Third grade (tertius gradus) — beginning the path
levels.stobaeus_grounding: 'the fool can perform appropriate actions but not right actions' (Ecl. 2.85)

levels.name: Deliberate
levels.description: Action from conscious reasoning, with some understanding of the rational foundation. Passion partially checked but still operative.
levels.progress_grade: Second grade (secundus gradus) — overcoming the worst

levels.name: Principled
levels.description: Action from stable commitment to virtue, with strong understanding and minimal passion. Approaching sage-like quality but not yet perfect.

levels.name: Sage-Like (Katorthoma)
levels.description: Action from perfected understanding and unified virtue. Complete freedom from destructive passion. The action is not merely appropriate but right.
levels.note: The gap between 'principled' and 'sage-like' is enormous. The Stoics acknowledged the sage was 'as rare as the phoenix.' This scale preserves that emphasis.

scoring_outputs.description: What the scoring engine produces for different types of evaluation. These correspond to SageReasoning's P5/P6/P7 workflows.

past_action.label: P6 — Scoring a completed action
outputs: control_filter: What was and was not within the agent's prohairesis
kathekon_assessment: Was this an appropriate action? (yes/no + quality)
passions_detected: Which specific passions distorted the causal sequence, with sub-species
false_judgements: The specific false beliefs that drove any passion
virtue_domains_engaged: Which of the four virtue expressions were relevant
katorthoma_proximity: Where on the 5-level scale
ruling_faculty_state: Stable disposition or temporary state
improvement_path: Which false judgement to correct, which passion to address
oikeiosis_obligations: Which social obligations were relevant and whether met

prospective_action.label: P7 — Advising on a proposed action
outputs: deliberation_framework: Applying Cicero's 5 questions from action.json
kathekon_assessment: Is the proposed action appropriate?
passion_risks: Which passions are likely to distort judgement in this situation
oikeiosis_check: Which social obligations are at stake
sage_framing: How a sage would see this situation (correct impression, correct assent, proportionate impulse)
recommended_action: The action that best expresses unified virtue

indifferents_ranking.label: P5 — Ranking competing external goals
outputs: selective_value_ranking: Ordered by axia from value.json
oikeiosis_alignment: Which option best serves current social obligations
passion_risk_assessment: Which option most likely to generate attachment
virtue_opportunity: Which option provides best material for virtuous action
sage_selection: What a sage would select (eklegetai) vs. desire (oregetai)
